disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of severe low back pain immediately after a trivial mechanical movement (bending to pick up a pen), with visible/palpable rock-hard tightening of the paraspinal muscles
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it started?
How did the pain come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I just bent forward to pick up a pen and my whole lower back seized up instantly — it's been locked solid ever since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No radiation of pain below the knee, no dermatomal leg pain, no pins-and-needles, no leg numbness or weakness
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain shoot down either leg past the knee?
Any pins and needles, numbness or weakness in your legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it stays in my back. Nothing shoots down my legs, no tingling or numbness, and my legs feel strong and normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No loss of bladder or bowel control, no urinary retention, and no numbness in the saddle/perineal region (cauda equina screen negative by history)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any trouble controlling your bladder or bowels, or trouble passing urine?
Any numbness around your groin, bottom, or inner thighs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. I can control my bladder and bowels fine and there's no numbness around my groin or bottom.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No major trauma, no history of cancer, no fevers, night sweats, or unexplained weight loss, and pain is not worse at night at rest
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, night sweats, or weight loss you can't explain?
Any history of cancer, or a fall or accident?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or sweats, no weight loss, no cancer history, and there was no fall — I only bent over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is completely immobile on the floor, lives alone, and cannot reach analgesia or transfer without physical assistance
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you who can help?
Are you able to get up or move at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm on my own and stuck on the floor. I can't get up and I can't even reach the cupboard for painkillers.
